In August 2021, Lincoln became the first club from Gibraltar to ever reach the group stages of a European club competition after defeating the Latvian champions [Riga FC](/wiki/Riga_FC "Riga FC") in the play-off round of the [UEFA Europa Conference League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Europa_Conference_League "2021–22 UEFA Europa Conference League").

**Lincoln Red Imps Football Club** is a professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club from [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar").[[6]](#cite_note-6) They play in the [Gibraltar Football League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_League "Gibraltar Football League"), and share [Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium,_Gibraltar "Victoria Stadium, Gibraltar") with all other clubs in the territory.[[7]](#cite_note-7) They are the country's record champion with 29 league titles, including fourteen consecutive titles between 2003 and 2016, and in 2014 were the first team to represent Gibraltar in the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League")'s qualifying stage. In August 2021, Lincoln became the first club from Gibraltar to ever reach the group stages of a European club competition after defeating the Latvian champions [Riga FC](/wiki/Riga_FC "Riga FC") in the play-off round of the [UEFA Europa Conference League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Europa_Conference_League "2021–22 UEFA Europa Conference League").

The club has won 7 [domestic trebles](/wiki/Domestic_treble "Domestic treble") and 4 [doubles](/wiki/Double_(association_football) "Double (association football)") in its history.[[8]](#cite_note-8) They hold the record for Europe's longest men's chronological unbeaten league run of 88 matches over 1,959 days from May 2009 to September 2014.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The football club was formed in 1976 by Charles Polson and managed by Charles Head . A group of players associated to the old Police youth team called the "Blue Batons" and complemented by players that had been released by [Glacis United](/wiki/Glacis_United_F.C. "Glacis United F.C.") and St Jago's joined forces to form the first Lincoln team that played in the Gibraltar fourth division as a youth team. The team was named after [Lincoln City FC](/wiki/Lincoln_City_FC "Lincoln City FC"), who are nicknamed the "Imps" after the legend of the [Lincoln Imp](/wiki/Lincoln_Imp "Lincoln Imp"). Lincoln Red Imps received their name after they were sponsored by Reg Brealey, the former [Sheffield United](/wiki/Sheffield_United "Sheffield United") chairman and [Lincoln](/wiki/Lincoln,_England "Lincoln, England") resident who is friends with Charles Polson.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
[[12]](#cite_note-12)

When this Lincoln team became old enough to play Senior Football, it was decided to place the young team into the GFA second division. The team started to climb to the top in the second division in the season 1981–82 winning the league, the division cup and gaining promotion to the GFA's first division after only one season. The next year Lincoln finished in mid table in the first division. In 1983–84 Lincoln won the league and promotion to the [Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Gibraltar Premier Division").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 1984–85 Lincoln played their first season in the Gibraltar Premier Division in which they were joint champions with [Glacis United](/wiki/Glacis_United_F.C. "Glacis United F.C.") – the first of seven league titles which were won between 1984 and 1994.

Lincoln won 14 Gibraltar Premier Division titles in a row from 2003 to 2016, bettering the previous record of nine in a row held by Glacis United in the 1960s. They also won the national treble of League, Rock Cup and Senior Cup in 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008 and 2011.

In 2014, after Gibraltar had joined UEFA as the 54th member, Lincoln became the first Gibraltarian team to play in the qualifying stages of the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League"). After claiming a 1–1 draw in the home match, they lost 5–2 at Faroese team [HB](/wiki/Havnar_B%C3%B3ltfelag "Havnar Bóltfelag") and were eliminated in the first qualifying round.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Lincoln won both the [2015 Rock Cup](/wiki/2015_Rock_Cup "2015 Rock Cup") with a 4–1 victory over [Lynx F.C.](/wiki/Lynx_F.C. "Lynx F.C.")[[15]](#cite_note-2015_Rock_Cup_Final_Match_Report-15) and the [2014–15 Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2014–15 Gibraltar Premier Division") championship by 16 points,[[16]](#cite_note-16) again playing in the qualification stages for the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") for with their second straight double.[[17]](#cite_note-Gibraltar_double-17) In the [First Qualifying Round](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_UEFA_Champions_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#First_qualifying_round "2015–16 UEFA Champions League qualifying phase and play-off round") Lincoln were drawn against [FC Santa Coloma](/wiki/FC_Santa_Coloma "FC Santa Coloma") of [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra "Andorra").[[18]](#cite_note-1st_Qualifying_Round-18) After a scoreless draw in the first leg in Gibraltar,[[19]](#cite_note-Red_Imps_vs._FC_Santa_Coloma_Match_Report_1-19) the club earned a 2–1 victory at the [Estadi Comunal d'Andorra la Vella](/wiki/Estadi_Comunal_d%27Andorra_la_Vella "Estadi Comunal d'Andorra la Vella") to advance to the [Second Qualifying Round](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_UEFA_Champions_League_qualifying_phase_and_play-off_round#First_qualifying_round "2015–16 UEFA Champions League qualifying phase and play-off round") with goals coming from [Anthony Bardon](/wiki/Anthony_Bardon "Anthony Bardon") and [Lee Casciaro](/wiki/Lee_Casciaro "Lee Casciaro").[[20]](#cite_note-Lincoln_Red_Imps_vs._FC_Santa_Coloma_Match_Report_2-20) With the victory, Lincoln became the first club from Gibraltar to reach the second round of UEFA Champions League qualification, setting up an encounter with [2015 Danish champions](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Danish_Superliga "2014–15 Danish Superliga") [FC Midtjylland](/wiki/FC_Midtjylland "FC Midtjylland"),[[21]](#cite_note-Lincoln_Make_History!-21) which Lincoln Red Imps lost on aggregate 0–3.

In 2016 Lincoln pulled off a surprise victory against [Scottish Premiership](/wiki/Scottish_Premiership "Scottish Premiership") champions [Celtic](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C."), with a [1–0 victory at home](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_1-0_Celtic "Lincoln Red Imps 1-0 Celtic") after a 48th-minute strike from [Lee Casciaro](/wiki/Lee_Casciaro "Lee Casciaro") in the club's second round match of the UEFA Champions League qualifying stage.
The match was [Brendan Rodgers](/wiki/Brendan_Rodgers "Brendan Rodgers") first competitive fixture as manager of Celtic.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The Glasgow side still comfortably qualified with a 3–0 victory in the return leg at Celtic Park. It is still seen as one of Celtic's worst moments.

On 26 August 2021, Lincoln became the first team from [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") to qualify for the group stage in a European Competition, as they defeated [Riga FC](/wiki/Riga_FC "Riga FC") by a score of 3–1 (4–2 aggregate) and advanced to the group stage of the [2021–22 UEFA Europa Conference League](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_UEFA_Europa_Conference_League "2021–22 UEFA Europa Conference League").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

On 28 August 2025, Lincoln Red Imps qualified for the [2025–26 UEFA Conference League](/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_UEFA_Conference_League "2025–26 UEFA Conference League") league phase after defeats in the [2025–26 UEFA Europa League](/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_UEFA_Europa_League "2025–26 UEFA Europa League") play-off round to [Braga](/wiki/S.C._Braga "S.C. Braga"), making it their second time in European group stage competition. On 23 October 2025, they made history by becoming the first team from Gibraltar to win a match in the European group stage by beating Polish champions, [Lech Poznań](/wiki/Lech_Pozna%C5%84 "Lech Poznań"), 2–1 at home.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C.&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Stadium")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/DSC_0286_C.jpg/250px-DSC_0286_C.jpg)](/wiki/File:DSC_0286_C.jpg)

[Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium,_Gibraltar "Victoria Stadium, Gibraltar")

Lincoln currently plays all league games at the 2,000-seat Victoria Stadium. They often play home games in front of hundreds of spectators. The Lincoln Red Imps share this ground with all clubs currently participating in the [Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Gibraltar Premier Division"), as well as the [Gibraltar Women's Football League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Women%27s_Football_League "Gibraltar Women's Football League"), of which Lincoln also runs a team.

Due to the ground's failure to meet guidelines in the 2017–18 season, Lincoln, along with [Europa](/wiki/Europa_F.C. "Europa F.C.") and [St Joseph's](/wiki/St_Joseph%27s_F.C. "St Joseph's F.C."), were forced to play their European competition matches at the [Estadio Algarve](/wiki/Estadio_Algarve "Estadio Algarve") in [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), while improvements were carried out on Victoria Stadium.

It is expected that the ground will be renovated and expanded to a football specific stadium with a capacity of 8,000 by 2021.[[25]](#cite_note-26)
